commit.message:
Simplify DinD DNS: use CoreDNS directly via nftables MASQUERADE

Now that nftables MASQUERADE works for 172.16.0.0/12, Docker containers
inside DinD can query K8s CoreDNS (10.43.0.10) directly. The MASQUERADE
rule rewrites the source IP (172.17.x.x → pod IP) so CoreDNS accepts the
queries. The socat UDP DNS proxy is no longer needed and has been removed.

The dind container now only needs nftables (no socat), simplifying startup.

README: add WIP notice, update architecture to reflect current state
(nftables MASQUERADE instead of socat DNS proxy, Ollama instead of Dynamo).

openshell gateway still fails 'Cluster DNS resolution failed' — under
investigation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(docker): add ca-certificates to debian.12 deps image

The non-root user commit added --no-install-recommends to apt-get, which
dropped ca-certificates (previously pulled in as a recommended dep of wget).
Without it, dotnet-install.sh can't verify TLS when fetching latest.version
from builds.dotnet.microsoft.com, causing the build to fail.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(lint): cover BrokenPaths::check_file root-path no-parent branch

Add a test that calls check_file with Path::new("/") backed by a
MockFs that returns content for "/". Because Path::new("/").parent()
returns None on Unix, this exercises the previously uncovered else-branch
of 'let Some(skill_dir) = skill.path.parent()' at line 103 in check_file.

Branch coverage for broken_paths.rs: 95.00% → 97.62%
Overall branch coverage: 92.74% → 92.79%

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
TarReader: implement GNU sparse format 1.0 (PAX) (#125283)

`TarReader` was not handling GNU sparse format 1.0 PAX entries, causing
~46% of entries from bsdtar-created archives (e.g., .NET SDK tarballs
built on macOS/APFS) to expose internal placeholder paths like
`GNUSparseFile.0/real-file.dll`, incorrect sizes, and corrupted
extracted content.

## Changes

Added read-only support for GNU sparse format 1.0 (PAX). When
`TarReader` encounters PAX extended attributes `GNU.sparse.major=1` and
`GNU.sparse.minor=0`, it resolves the real file name from
`GNU.sparse.name`, reports the expanded size from `GNU.sparse.realsize`,
and wraps the raw data stream with `GnuSparseStream` which presents the
expanded virtual file content (zeros for holes, packed data at correct
offsets).

The sparse map embedded in the data section is parsed **lazily** on
first `Read`, so `_dataStream` remains unconsumed during entry
construction. This allows `TarWriter.WriteEntry` to round-trip the
condensed sparse data correctly for both seekable and non-seekable
source archives.

Older GNU sparse formats (0.0, 0.1) and write support are not addressed.

Additional correctness and robustness improvements based on code review:
- `GnuSparseStream` now overrides `DisposeAsync` to properly await async
disposal of the underlying raw stream.
- `TarHeader.Read` now throws `InvalidDataException` if
`GNU.sparse.realsize` is negative, consistent with validation of the
regular `_size` field.
- Segment validation uses overflow-safe arithmetic (`offset > _realSize
|| length > _realSize - offset`).
- `FindSegmentFromCurrent` uses binary search (O(log n)) for backward
seeks, preserving the O(1) amortized forward scan for the common
sequential-read case.

```csharp
// Before: entry.Name == "GNUSparseFile.0/dotnet.dll", entry.Length == 512
// After:  entry.Name == "dotnet.dll", entry.Length == 1048576
using var reader = new TarReader(archiveStream);
TarEntry entry = reader.GetNextEntry();
entry.DataStream.ReadExactly(content); // correctly expanded virtual file
```

## Testing

All existing tests pass. New `TarReader.SparseFile.Tests.cs` covers:
- Parameterized sparse layouts (single segment, holes, multiple
segments, all-holes) × `copyData` × sync/async
- Corrupted sparse map handling (non-numeric values, truncated maps,
buffer overflow) × sync/async
- Negative `GNU.sparse.realsize` value throws `InvalidDataException`
(sync and async) — the test helper `WriteSparseEntry` omits
`GNU.sparse.realsize` from the `PaxTarEntry` constructor's attribute
dictionary (to avoid constructor-level validation) and instead injects
it via reflection into the internal `TarHeader.ExtendedAttributes`
dictionary after construction, so the archive can be built while
ensuring `TarReader.GetNextEntry()` is the one that throws
- Wrong sparse version detection (missing minor, wrong major)
- Seekable random access, partial reads, advance-past-entry correctness
- Round-trip copy through TarWriter with seekable/non-seekable source ×
copyData
- Sparse layout scenarios tested against real `golang_tar` test data
files (`pax-nil-sparse-data.tar`, `pax-nil-sparse-hole.tar`,
`pax-sparse-big.tar`) from the `System.Formats.Tar.TestData` NuGet
package, plus programmatically constructed archives for additional
coverage
- Test code refactored to eliminate duplication:
`AdvancePastEntry_DoesNotCorruptNextEntry` and
`CopySparseEntryToNewArchive_PreservesExpandedContent` now share archive
construction helpers (`WriteSparseEntry`, `BuildSparseArchive`,
`BuildRawSparseArchive`) with the rest of the test suite

commit.message:
Filter out no-identifier Open Library records from slot extraction

Exclude OL edition records that lack both ISBN and LCCN from all
three slot mining queries (strict pattern matches, loose the-X-of-Y,
loose comma-list). These tend to be dissertations, pamphlets, and
government reports whose language doesn't reflect real published-book
subtitles.

Impact: ~5-6% trim of the niche tail (~460 fillers), zero impact on
pop/mainstream tiers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add support for importing legacy datumaro datasets with videos (#2093)

This PR adds support for importing legacy datumaro datasets with videos.
It includes new converters for unified media handling and enhancements
to the legacy dataset conversion logic.

**New converters and media support:**

* Added `MediaPathToMediaInfoConverter` and
`VideoFramePathToMediaPathConverter` to enable unified metadata
extraction and field promotion for both image and video media
* Introduced `ForwardVideoMediaConverter` and
`ForwardMixedMediaConverter` for handling conversion of legacy video
frame and mixed media datasets, including logic to avoid duplicate
entries for videos with annotated frames
* Unannotated videos will get an empty annotation at frame 0 as there is
no way currently to represent a full unannotated video in a dataset.

commit.message:
Fix converter tracking in pickled dataset and COCO category issues (#2100)

This PR fixes an issue where a converter could be applied twice if a
dataset was pickled. Full details in
https://github.com/open-edge-platform/training_extensions/issues/6048

Also fixes a bug where imported COCO datasets could pickup only a small
set of the available labels incase there are multiple annotation files.

* Add @var++, @var--, @var+=, @var-= shorthand operators

Co-authored-by: KeanuReadmes <265799433+KeanuReadmes@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(coreutils/ls): use modern Corvo shorthand operators

- Replace map.get(@parsed, ...) with bracket access @parsed["key"]
- Replace map.get(@o, "flag", false) assignments with or= operator
- Replace map.set(map.set(...)) chains with bracket assignment @m["k"] = v
- Collapse follow/fmt_C/ww/hides/ignores/sort-key flag init using or=
- Simplify assert_eq(map.get(@o, "flag", false), true) → assert_eq(@o["flag"], true)
- Simplify map.get(ee, "field") reads to bracket access ee["field"]
- Convert string.concat chains in total/header/line-assembly to interpolation
- Use += for string append (pre_ln, ino_s, blk_s, ipr, namel)
- Use intermediate @_maj/@_min/@_tgt vars to avoid nested quotes
- Collapse 9-step p1..pd chain into single interpolated string
- Remove redundant @suf = "" in classify fallback (initialized above)

* docs: rewrite README.md from scratch

Highlight three core paradigms:
- Zero dependencies / zero supply-chain risk (no imports, no package manager)
- Procedural: no if/else, no function declarations (try/fallback, match, procedures)
- Real async backed by Rust (async_browse)

All examples use @shorthand notation. Corvo image retained.
Covers: stdlib table, compile-to-binary, prep blocks, async_browse,
browse, loop, assertions, subprocess, CLI reference, error codes.

fix(pwa): prevent update banner from re-appearing after reload

- Added sessionStorage flag 'pwa-just-updated' to track if updated this session
- Guard in checkForUpdates() and statechange handler to skip showing banner if flag set
- Set flag in updateApp() before calling postMessage({ type: 'SKIP_WAITING' })
- Flag persists through page reload but clears after 10s
- Allows detection of new updates if user reloads again later

Fixes: banner appearing again after user completes update and page reloads

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: salva_settimana_con_nome usa PostgreSQL invece di JSON

Quando si crea una settimana personalizzata e si salva con un nome,
il codice stava scrivendo su settimane_salvate.json (legacy)
Ora usa save_settimana() per salvare nel database PostgreSQL

Questo risolvere il bug dove le settimane create non venivano salvate

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: side-by-side toggleable panels for Document, Raw JSON, Event Graph

Replace tab switching with toggle buttons — any combination of panels
can be shown simultaneously in columns. Panels divide space equally
with divider borders between them.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix pr-docs-check: remove invalid discussions permission (surgical) (#16000)

The gh-aw compiler (v0.67.2) unconditionally adds 'discussions: write'
to the conclusion and safe_outputs job permissions and
'permission-discussions: write' to the create-github-app-token inputs.
This is not a valid GitHub Apps API permission scope, causing token
minting to fail with HTTP 422.

This commit surgically removes only the 4 invalid lines from the
existing compiled lock file — no recompile, so all other fixes from
PRs #15923, #15929, #15943, #15960 are preserved.

Also adds:
- .github/aw/patch-lock.sh: helper to re-apply this patch after
  future recompiles
- Comment in the .md source documenting the workaround

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(pwa): lock banner UI during update to prevent user interference

- Added guard in handleDismissUpdate() to prevent dismiss when isUpdating=true
- Added disabled={isUpdating} to close button (X)
- Added disabled styles to close button: opacity-40, cursor-not-allowed
- Update button already had disabled={isUpdating} to prevent double-click
- When updating, banner is now fully locked: cannot close, update spinner shows

Improves UX: prevents user from dismissing/closing banner while SW activates

commit.message:
Fix SolutionPersistence version overrides to match MSFT build (#1631)

Add /p:Version override so ProductVersion reports 1.0.52 instead of
1.0.0 (Nerdbank.GitVersioning fallback). Add FileVersionRevision=6595
so FileVersion is 1.0.52.6595 matching the official package. Rename
version property to VsSolutionPersistenceReleaseVersion to follow the
convention expected by the update-external-metadata script.

Update submodule to v1.0.52 (19c3ca7) and remove the now-redundant
C# 13 language version patch that is already included upstream.

commit.message:
perf(db): add composite indexes for common query patterns

Add 7 composite indexes to improve query performance:
- Homework: [sectionId, submissionDueAt] for section homework list
- HomeworkAuditLog: [sectionId, createdAt] for audit log queries
- Comment: [status, createdAt] for admin moderation queue
- Comment: [rootId, createdAt] for thread chronological display
- Upload: [userId, createdAt] for user upload list
- DescriptionEdit: [descriptionId, createdAt] for edit history
- Todo: [userId, completed, dueAt] for user todo sorting

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
